In his second question, the moderator tries to bait JD Vance into criticising Ukraine, as the chair asks about what he says are “Ukrainian intelligence services attempting to influence” elections in the US or Hungary. “I’ve also been told that the vice-president of the United States coming and saying that Viktor Orbán is doing a good job

stories.title: Russian spooks hack Wi-Fi routers to spy on West
stories.summary: Russia’s elite hacking group Fancy Bear is behind a large-scale campaign to spy on militaries and governments by hacking Wi-Fi routers, security agencies in the United States and Europe have said. Intelligence and law enforcement services in the U.S., Canada, Ukraine, Germany, Italy, Poland and others exposed a large-scale operation by the Russian hacking group spying through ill-protected Wi-Fi routers, in a joint statement published late Tuesday. stories.title: Hegseth declares victory in Iran but says US forces will remain in region
stories.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth claimed Wednesday that American forces have secured a “historic and overwhelming victory” against Iran but said that military assets will remain in the region for now to ensure Tehran’s new leaders comply with the terms of a tentative peace deal between the nations. “We’ll be hanging around. We’re not going anywhere,” Hegseth said during a Pentagon press conference a day after President Donald Trump announced a two-week pause in fighting amid peace negotiations. “O

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Kyiv lays out how ‘Russian satellites help Iran in war’
stories.summary: Iran bombed US bases and allies’ facilities soon after Russian satellites mapped them, according to Ukrainian assessment. What we know on day 1,505 Russian satellites made detailed imagery of military facilities and critical sites across the Middle East including US bases and other targets that were attacked by Iran soon afterwards , according to a Ukrainian intelligence assessment. Reuters reported that the assessment cited at least 24 surveys of areas in 11 Middle Eastern countries from 21-31

stories.title: A new generation of politicians of colour is emerging in France. The backlash speaks volumes
stories.summary: Local elections have led to a surge of racism in a country that still struggles to see itself as anything other than white Saint-Denis is just over 9km from the centre of Paris but is in the poorest department in all of metropolitan France, a region marked by unemployment, low incomes and social disadvantage. But Saint Denis’s town hall was the backdrop to memorably joyous celebrations on the evening of 15 March. A delirious crowd carried the new mayor shoulder high, chanting his name over and o

stories.title: Iran war shows Trump not ‘reliable ally’ for UK, says Ed Davey – UK politics live
stories.summary: Lib Dem leader says conflict shows ‘classic Donald Trump cycle of destruction’ OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT, is proposing the extension of the four-day working week , as a response to AI taking over some of the work done by humans. But for the Conservative party the four-day working week, at least in the public sector, is viewed as a menace. Officially, that’s a value-for-money position, but it also overlaps with their opposition to civil servants working from home, which has some of the t

stories.title: Germany accuses JD Vance of hypocrisy over claim that EU meddled in Hungary’s election
stories.summary: BERLIN — Germany on Wednesday chided JD Vance for hypocrisy, as it pushed back against the U.S. vice president’s accusation that Brussels was meddling in Hungary’s election. Berlin said that, on the contrary, Vance’s visit to Budapest shortly before Sunday’s vote suggests that he himself may be guilty of election interference, as he vigorously boosted the populist-nationalist government. “We reject the accusation made by U.S. Vice President JD Vance at an event in Hungary

stories.title: Greece proposes law to keep kids off social media
stories.summary: ATHENS — The Greek government is set to ban children under the age of 15 from using social media platforms. Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis announced the draft law by addressing young people directly in a TikTok video, where he accused “the addictive design of some apps” and their “profit model that’s based on your attention” of taking away “some of your innocence and freedom.” “I am certain that many of you … will be angry with me,” he added. Under t

stories.title: ‘A full-on embrace’: how the EU’s largest news publisher fell in love with the US
stories.summary: After recent purchase of UK’s Daily Telegraph, Axel Springer and its ‘guru-like’ CEO, Mathias Döpfner, have sights on transatlantic expansion In Mathias Döpfner’s 2023 book Dealing with Dictators, the chief executive of the German media company Axel Springer SE proposed a fix for western democracy: states that respect the rule of law should stick together and prioritise trading with each other. Better that, he declared, than indulging the illusion that doing business will tame “self-styled stron

stories.title: Serbia looks for pipeline sabotage plot culprit as Vance's Hungary visit nears
stories.summary: Serbia has opened a probe into the attempted pipeline sabotage after explosives were found near Kanjiža, as military intelligence suspects a migrant with training, raising the temperature before a key election in Hungary on Sunday.

stories.title: Hungarians living in Ukraine caught between front lines
stories.summary: About 80,000 ethnic Hungarians live in western Ukraine. Many feel torn between Viktor Orban's anti-Ukrainian campaign and the conflict between the two countries. What does the future hold for this minority?

stories.title: Moscow threatens Baltic states, claiming they help Ukraine strike Russia
stories.summary: The Russian foreign ministry issued veiled threats to Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania, accusing them of assisting Ukraine in Kyiv’s campaign against Russia’s oil terminals.

stories.title: Szijjártó on Orbán-Putin transcript: If this isn't interference by the intelligence services, then what is?
stories.summary: The minister does not know where the “telephone wiretaps and transcripts obtained using secret service methods” came from, because they do not possess such records.

stories.title: "The Defense Minister made me three offers to stop me from speaking out"
stories.summary: In his second interview with Telex, Szilveszter Pálinkás responded to the reactions to him speaking up about the dire state of the Hungarian Armed Forces, and spoke about how his fellow soldiers responded.

stories.title: Billions in state funds secretly channeled to Viktor Orbán’s favourite company before defense acquisitions
stories.summary: According to documents obtained by Direkt36, state funds were secretly directed to a company called 4iG. This happened at the very time when the government decided to sell state-owned defense companies to this firm.

stories.summary: Baby was delivered during Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston to the US; nationality of child to be determined Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A routine passenger flight from Jamaica landed at New York ’s John F Kennedy international airport with one more person than it took off with after a woman gave birth in midair, potentially setting up a tricky situation over the newborn’s citizenship. The “medical event” occurred on a Caribbean Airlines fl

stories.title: Vance’s whirlwind visit may not help Orbán to the election victory he craves
stories.summary: As the US vice-president wades into a heated campaign, Hungary’s leader faces the real possibility of defeat Even before the plane carrying JD and Usha Vance had landed in Budapest, the Hungarian government had hailed their two-day visit as a new golden age in the relationship between Washington and Budapest. What came next was a whirlwind of politics in which the US vice-president waded directly into the country’s heated election campaign, just days before Hungarians cast their ballots. Continu

stories.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
stories.summary: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

stories.title: Orbán offered help to Putin 'in any way that I can,' reports say
stories.summary: Orbán offered his support to Putin, likening his assistance to that of a mouse helping a lion in a well-known Hungarian fable, according to a phone conversation transcript obtained by news agency Bloomberg.

stories.title: JD Vance backs Orbán's re-election bid in Budapest visit and hits out at EU
stories.summary: US Vice-President JD Vance intervenes in the campaign to give Orbán a ringing endorsement in Budapest.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Ahhoz, hogy eldöntsük, ki lehet-e vezetni a veszélyhelyzetet, előbb látnunk kell, mi a valóság
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt vezetője azt mondta, nem számolnak a katonák tömeges leszerelésével.

stories.title: Így spórolhat fogászati kezelésein az egészségpénztárral
stories.summary: A számláikról elköltött pénz több mint egyharmadát a magánegészségügyben használják fel az egészségpénztári tagok a BiztosDöntés.hu által összegyűjtött adatok szerint: ennek az összegnek pedig a jelentős szeletét fogászati kezelésekre költik. Az egészségszámlákon felhalmozott megtakarítás pedig mind a megelőző jellegű ellátás, mind a nagyobb lélegzetű kezelések finanszírozásánál nagyon jól jöhet – amellett, hogy pénzben mérhető előnyökkel is jár.

stories.title: Itt működünk, adózunk, több mint 710 milliárdot fizettünk a költségvetésbe (x)
stories.summary: A hat nagy nemzetközi hátterű élelmiszer kiskereskedelmi lánc mindegyike maradéktalanul kiveszi a részét a magyar közteherviselésből. Ezek a cégek 2024-ben együttesen több mint 710 milliárd forint adót fizettek be a magyar költségvetésbe, ami önmagában is fedezné például a háziorvosi ellátás és pótlék együttes költségét, vagy a rendőrség éves fenntartását bérekkel együtt.

stories.title: 8 érv a Vista mellett, ha családi otthont keresünk
stories.summary: A családi otthon kiválasztásánál nemcsak a négyzetméterek számítanak, hanem az is, mi veszi körül a lakást: biztonságos közeg, jó iskolák és óvodák, elérhető parkok, játszóterek, működő infrastruktúra. A Foka-öbölnél hamarosan műszaki átadási fázisba kerül a Duna Terasz Vista – a több száz lakásos fejlesztés mögött nemcsak prémium lokáció, hanem családbarát szemlélet és komoly építészeti teljesítmény is áll.

stories.title: Magyar Péter győzelme lett a befektetők általános várakozása a nemzetközi piacokon
stories.summary: A legtöbben arra fogadnak, nyerni fog a Tisza Párt vasárnap, így a forint erősödésére és a magyar gazdaság fellendülésére számítanak.

stories.title: Magyar Péter az országjárásán megjelenő orosz tudósítónak: Élvezze a szabadságot és a rendszerváltást!
stories.summary: A Tisza kiskunlacházai rendezvényén egy orosz lap újságíróját is kiszúrták, Magyar angolul köszöntötte.

stories.title: A Fidesznek nagyon fontosak lettek a választókerületi viták, hogy elmondhassák: a tiszások menekülnek
stories.summary: Magyar Péter szerint ellentmondásos, hogy Orbán Viktor őt bábnak tartja, ezért nem áll ki vele vitázni, közben a fideszes jelöltek országszerte vitákra várják a tiszásokat. Ők a miniszterelnök-jelölti vita hiányában egyikre se mennek el, a Fidesz így a DK és a Mi Hazánk jelöltjeivel vitázik országszerte. A DK azzal indokolta a részvételét, hogy nem lehet gyávasággal küzdeni a kormány ellen.

stories.title: Csúcsra ugrott a forint az iráni tűzszünettől
stories.summary: A legfontosabb magyar részvények is szárnyalnak a Budapesti Értéktőzsdén.

stories.title: Rácz András az Orbán–Putyin telefonbeszélgetésről: A magyar miniszterelnök porig alázta saját magát és az országot is
stories.summary: Az Oroszország-szakértő szerint a tavaly októberi telefonhívás „a szervilizmus demonstrálása” volt Orbán részéről.

stories.title: Politico: 12 pontos együttműködési tervet írt alá Szijjártó az oroszokkal, az orosz nyelv hazai erősítése is felmerült benne
stories.summary: A lap szerint felvetődött az is, hogy megfontolják oroszországi tanárok hazai alkalmazását.

stories.title: Az MNB-ügyből megismert francia ingatlanos nagyágyú fejlesztene Budapest egyik legígéretesebb területén
stories.summary: A svájci Ultima nevű luxusingatlanos cég alapítója, Max-Hervé George egy magyar gigaprojekt körül is feltűnik. Kérdés, hogyan találta meg magának a projektet, amit állami együttműködés nélkül aligha lehet végigvinni.

stories.title: Egy nappal tovább marad az amerikai alelnök, Budapesten több utat is lezárnak
stories.summary: Csütörtökön pedig a repülőteret lesz nehéz megközelíteni, érdemes lesz korábban elindulni.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a Medián friss felméréséről: Senki ne hagyja magát elaltatni, nem közvélemény-kutatást kell nyerni
stories.summary: A Medián stabil kétharmadot becsült a Tiszának, Magyar Péter szerint ebből lehet kétharmados Tisza-győzelem, és nagyon gyenge győzelem is.

stories.summary: A Partizán, a Roma Sajtóközpont és a Bura Galéria munkatársa 42 éves volt.

stories.title: Pénzt kaptam Orbánéktól, hogy jó adófizetőket neveljek az országnak. Ez pont arra elég, hogy rendszeresen kirepüljek hozzájuk Berlinbe
stories.summary: „Óriási boldogság, hogy összesen négy generációért imádkozhatok. De ha a szüleim lerobbannak, elég nagy bajban leszek” – idős szüleihez ingázik vidékre, kamasz gyerekét neveli és unokáira vigyáz. Az 56 éves Rita történetén keresztül mutatjuk be, milyen problémákkal küzd a szendvicsgeneráció, és hogyan hatott rájuk a NER 16 éve.

stories.title: Vance Budapesten: Orbán Viktor tette a legtöbbet a békéért
stories.summary: Az amerikai alelnök az MCC-ben üzent Iránnak is, remélve, hogy jó döntést hoz a vezetés a tűzszünet után. J. D. Vance szerint az európai vezetők sok rossz döntést hoztak, a kontinens ezért van nehezebb helyzetben, mint az Egyesült Államok.

stories.title: Kollégánk, Vorák Anita is elnyerte a MÚOSZ sajtódíját az Orbán eseményeire szervezett skandálókról szóló anyagáért
stories.summary: A MÚOSZ négy anyagot is díjazott márciusban, mivel a szervezet szerint mindegyik megérdemli az elismerést.

stories.title: Ha Orbán veszít, oda a támogatások – ezzel buzdítják szavazásra a csángókat egy helyi szerint
stories.summary: A moldvai csángók lakta Gajcsánáról fordult egy civil szervezethez egy választó. Levelében részletesen leírja, hogyan bombázzák politikai üzenetekkel a helyieket, hogy leadják a levélszavazataikat. A csángószövetség elnöke nem is érti, miről beszél.

stories.title: A Telexnél folytatja Tamás Bence Gáspár
stories.summary: A magyar internetes videós újságírás egyik megteremtője saját anyagok készítése és szerkesztése mellett a lap kreatív folyamataiban is részt vesz majd.

stories.title: Vučić a robbanószerekről: Hallgatunk, amíg a magyarországi választások le nem zárulnak
stories.summary: Szégyellje magát, aki azt állítja, hogy a szerb hadsereg be akar avatkozni a magyar választásokba – mondta a szerb elnök. Amíg nem tudják biztosan, mi történt, nem osztanak meg részleteket a Török Áramlatnál talált robbanószerről.

stories.title: Hangfelvételekből kiderült, hogy Szijjártó hogyan egyeztetett Lavrovval Orbán moszkvai útja előtt, és milyen segítséget ígért az orosz külügyminiszternek
stories.summary: A külgazdasági és külügyminiszter arról is faggatta Szergej Lavrovot, hogy hogyan sikerült Putyin és Trump alaszkai találkozója.

stories.title: Serbia, Hungary say explosives found at Russian gas pipeline
stories.summary: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said detonators and explosives "of devastating power" were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline, which runs through Serbia and provides Russian gas to Hungary.

stories.title: Hungarian Easter tradition sees women doused with water in Hollókő
stories.summary: A centuries-old Easter tradition continues in a Hungarian village, where men pour water over women in a ritual rooted in folklore and springtime customs.

stories.title: Hungary's government accused of spying on opposition
stories.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban's government used the country's intelligence service to spy on the opposition Tisza Party, says investigative platform Direkt36. What does this mean for the upcoming parliamentary election?

stories.title: In Estonia, rumors are circulating that the city of Narva wants to secede and join Russia. Is it true?
stories.summary: The Estonian city of Narva is on Europe's eastern border, right next to Russia. Recently there have been online rumors that some locals want to secede from Estonia and join Russia. What's really happening?

stories.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
stories.summary: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National

stories.title: Europe cheers Trump’s Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: Europe breathed a cautious sigh of relief Wednesday after U.S. President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran, halting missile strikes in exchange for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen welcomed the agreement as “much-needed de-escalation,” thanking Pakistan for its mediation and calling for negotiations on an “enduring solution” to continue. “The U.S.–Iran agreement on a ceasefire is a step back from the brink,” EU foreign

stories.title: Oil prices plunge and stocks jump after Trump announces conditional ceasefire with Iran
stories.summary: Oil poised for biggest daily fall since pandemic as Iran says it will reopen strait of Hormuz under its management Middle East crisis – live updates US and Iran agree to provisional ceasefire as Tehran says it will reopen strait of Hormuz Oil prices plunged by almost 15% and global stock markets have rallied sharply after the US and Iran agreed a two-week conditional ceasefire . Investors hailed the news that Donald Trump had held off on his threat to bomb Iran into “the stone ages”, with Iran’s

stories.title: Turkey: DW correspondent Alican Uludag still in custody
stories.summary: Court reporter Alican Uludag has been in pre-trial detention in Istanbul for six weeks, charged with "insulting the president." His lawyers say this is completely disproportionate.

stories.title: Germany news: Military travel rule for young men put on hold
stories.summary: Germany's defense minister says a requirement for men aged 17 to 45 to seek permission for long stays abroad will not apply — for now. Meanwhile, German intelligence has a warning about your internet router. DW has more.

stories.title: Macron insists that Iran ceasefire must apply to Lebanon as well
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron said it is vital that the two-week ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran also extends to Israel’s strikes against Lebanon. “The situation is critical” in Lebanon, Macron warned Wednesday ahead of a meeting of the French defense council. “What we have witnessed with Israel’s strikes and occupation of southern Lebanon isn’t the right response,” he said. Israel has repeatedly struck Lebanon after an attack by the Iranian-affiliated Hezbollah group in March

stories.title: France aims to spend €76.3B on defense in 2030
stories.summary: PARIS — The French government presented its updated military planning law Wednesday that earmarks an extra €36 billion for defense by 2030. “The goal is for our armed forces to be capable of facing a potential major deployment within a timeframe that no one can predict,” French Armed Forces Minister Catherine Vautrin told reporters. “There will be additional orders of all types of munitions,” she added. The bill allocates an overall €63.3 billion for defense in 2027, €68.

stories.title: Chris Taylor wins Wisconsin state supreme court election giving liberal judges a 5-2 majority
stories.summary: Taylor defeated conservative rival Maria Lazar, providing another gauge of Democrats’ durability in midterms Wisconsin voters sent another liberal justice to the state supreme court, with Chris Taylor beating conservative Maria Lazar and giving liberals a 5-2 edge on the high court. The retirement of Justice Rebecca Bradley, a conservative, gave liberals a chance to further consolidate their hold on the high court ahead of the next presidential election, when the swing state is sure to see chall

stories.title: Germans pining for Gerhard Schröder forget his errors and ties to Putin. The SPD needs a fresh approach | Katja Hoyer
stories.summary: The former chancellor was the chief architect of Germany’s dependence on Russian fossil fuels and cuts to defence spending. Both haunt the country today The former German chancellor, Gerhard Schröder, is enjoying a curious political revival. Not so long ago, his reputation seemed in tatters. In light of the full-scale invasion of Ukraine, many came to regard his longstanding ties to Russia and personal friendship with Vladimir Putin as self-serving. Fellow Social Democrats (SPD) tried to expel h

stories.summary: Australia’s richest person questions cost and time spent investigating former soldiers as pockets of support emerge for Victoria Cross recipient Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s richest person, Gina Rinehart, says “I don’t understand” the justification for prosecuting Ben Roberts-Smith for alleged war crimes, as pockets of high-profile support emerge for the Victoria Cross recipient. Roberts-Smith was

stories.title: Keir Starmer heads to Gulf after US and Iran agree two-week ceasefire
stories.summary: PM to meet regional leaders to discuss effort to ‘support and sustain ceasefire’ and reopening the strait of Hormuz Middle East crisis – live updates Keir Starmer is travelling to the Gulf to meet leaders in the region to discuss diplomatic efforts to support the ceasefire agreed between the US and Iran. stories.summary: Announcement of deal met with relief, and calls for strait of Hormuz to be reopened and permanent end to hostilities US and Iran agree to provisional ceasefire Middle East crisis – live updates European leaders have welcomed the US-Iran ceasefire deal, while calling for the reopening of the strait of Hormuz and a permanent end to hostilities, including in Lebanon. stories.title: Londoners may regret protest votes for Reform or Greens in local elections, says Sadiq Khan
stories.summary: Exclusive: Capital’s mayor warns people not to use next month’s elections as a referendum on Labour’s progress UK politics live – latest updates Sadiq Khan has said he can understand why some former Labour voters are “flirting” with other parties in the run-up to May’s elections, but said that they may regret seeing a Green or Reform-led council in their areas. Speaking to the Guardian at a youth centre, where he was announcing new funding for facilities for young people, the London mayor also c

stories.summary: US defence secretary gives press briefing after provisional ceasefire agreed US and Iran agree to provisional ceasefire with Tehran saying it will reopen strait of Hormuz What is in Iran’s 10-point ceasefire plan and will the US agree to it? A genocidal threat, and then the US president, Donald Trump, blinked – without any apparently meaningful concessions from Iran. stories.title: ‘We still deserve due process,’ says Cambodian man deported by US to Eswatini
stories.summary: Pheap Rom was one of 15 people sent to prison in African kingdom last year despite completing US sentences A Cambodian man deported by the US said he would have accepted being sent to Cambodia, but instead ended up imprisoned in Eswatini, a country he knew so little about that when he first read the name he thought it was another immigration detention centre in Louisiana. Pheap Rom, who had been convicted of attempted murder, was one of 10 deportees sent to Eswatini by the US in October 2025. Th

stories.title: First Nation asks court to block Alberta referendum on seceding from Canada
stories.summary: Sturgeon Lake Cree Nation has asked a court to halt the separatist push, arguing it would violate their treaty rights A First Nation in Alberta has said that a separatist push for the province to secede from Canada is “consummately irresponsible and dishonourable” and should be shut down, arguing in court that a proposed referendum would violate their treaty rights. A minority of residents of the oil-rich province have long argued that the province’s woes are due to the structure of payments to

stories.title: US seeks to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia despite new Costa Rica deal
stories.summary: Man born in El Salvador has been fighting removal to series of ‘third’ countries after mistaken deportation last year US government attorneys on Tuesday told a federal judge the Department of Homeland Security still intends to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia, despite a new agreement with Costa Rica to accept deportees who cannot legally be returned to their home countries. The Salvadorian national’s case has become a focal point in the immigration debate after he was mistakenly deported t

stories.title: India news: Assam, Kerala, Puducherry gear up for elections
stories.summary: Voters across two states and one union territory in India will cast their ballot in Assembly elections tomorrow.

stories.title: Iranians form human chains on bridges amid tensions with US and Israel
stories.summary: Hundreds of people in Iran formed human chains on bridges after a government call to show support and protect infrastructure during heightened tensions.

stories.title: Five teens to go on trial in Sweden over attempted murder of Iranian dissident
stories.summary: Sweden’s security service Sapo says Iran has ramped up its targeting of dissidents since the start of the Iran war, accusing them of conscripting organised crime groups to carry out “acts of violence” on Swedish soil.

stories.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles after declaring South 'most hostile enemy'
stories.summary: The back-to-back launches came after Pyongyang made it clear that it had no intention of improving ties with Seoul, whose government steadfastly expressed its hopes of restoring long-dormant dialogue.

stories.title: Russia provided Iran with intelligence on Israeli energy sites, Ukraine says
stories.summary: Kyiv stated that Moscow is sharing its satellite intelligence with Iran to help Tehran strike Israel’s energy sites, which could cause power grid collapse and mass blackouts.

stories.title: Indian migrant workers hit by cooking gas shortages are leaving cities
stories.summary: Migrant workers say they are struggling to access cooking gas cylinders as the Iran war squeezes supplies.

stories.title: Newlywed wife of US soldier freed by ICE after detention at military base
stories.summary: Annie Ramos, an undocumented immigrant who came to the US as a toddler, had gone to the base to complete paperwork so the couple could move in together.

stories.title: UK picks new online safety watchdog
stories.summary: LONDON — The U.K. government has named Ian Cheshire, former chair of Channel 4, as its preferred candidate as chair of the media regulator Ofcom. Cheshire’s appointment is still subject to a parliamentary hearing by the Science, Innovation and Technology Select Committee, which is chaired by former Ofcom staffer Chi Onwurah. Cheshire was most recently the chair of Channel 4 up until April last year. Previously he was chief executive of retail group Kingfisher, and held non-executive and advisory

stories.title: Turkey to race ahead of EU on battery storage amid fossil fuel crisis
stories.summary: More than 33GW of battery capacity approved for Turkish grid since 2022 compared with 12-13GW in Germany Turkey has given the green light to more batteries to buffer its electricity grid than any EU member state, a report has found, in a further sign of rich countries losing steam in the race to a clean economy. More than 33GW of battery capacity have been approved in Turkey since 2022, according to the climate thinktank Ember, while the total planned and operational capacity in European frontru

stories.title: From the archive: Freedom without constraints: how the US squandered its cold war victory – podcast
stories.summary: We are raiding the Guardian long read archives to bring you some classic pieces from years past, with new introductions from the authors. stories.summary: Chris Bowen says Australians should ‘not get ahead of ourselves’ by expecting prices to fall Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Track current petrol and diesel prices across Australia Petrol prices are rising again and diesel wholesale prices have hit new record highs as the government warns the US ceasefire with Iran will not make fuel cheaper. stories.title: Tropical Cyclone Vaianu may bring life-threatening winds to New Zealand, forecasters warn
stories.summary: Category 3 cyclone is moving south of Fiji towards New Zealand, with winds at centre in excess of 150km/h Tropical Cyclone Vaianu forming in the Pacific could bring life-threatening winds and heavy rain to New Zealand later this week, forecasters have said, with strong wind watches issued for the entire North Island. The category 3 cyclone is moving south of Fiji towards New Zealand, with winds around the centre in excess of 150km/h, MetService said on Wednesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Europe cannot bet on a post-Trump US turning back to sanity | Rafael Behr
stories.summary: If the president’s first term didn’t inoculate the American body politic against tyranny, there is no guarantee that a second dose will work Donald Trump is a despot and the US is a democracy. These things can be true simultaneously but not indefinitely. There is now deadlock in the struggle between a president who would be king and a constitution drafted in repudiation of monarchy. But it is a battle to the death. Tyranny will either break the spirit of the republic or be quelled by it. Since t

stories.summary: Qarsoq Høegh-Dam aims to use his seat in Danish parliament to shift power from Copenhagen to Nuuk It’s not the standard motto for a newly elected parliamentarian, but Qarsoq Høegh-Dam is adamant: if he does his job properly, there will soon be no need for it. “I want to make myself as obsolete as possible,” he said. Last month, Høegh-Dam, a Greenlandic politician, became the first member of the pro-independence Naleraq to be elected to the Danish parliament. The new MP is clear that if all goes

stories.title: Family pay tribute to British teenager killed in motorcycle crash in Vietnam
stories.summary: Orla Wates, 19, who died after incident on popular Ha Giang loop, described as ‘beautiful, independent and very funny’ The family of a British teenager have paid tribute to their daughter who died after a motorcycle crash on a popular route in Vietnam. The incident occurred on the Ha Giang loop in the country’s north, and Orla Wates, 19, died at the Viet Duc university hospital in Hanoi, according to Viet Nam News. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ceasefire wins Trump instant gratification but Iran can enter talks with stronger hand

stories.title: ‘His last gift’: father dies saving two of his children from drowning off Florida coast
stories.summary: Maine family was on vacation when Ryan Jennings died saving his son and daughter from rip current off Juno Beach A Maine family is grieving after a father died recently saving his son and one of his daughters from drowning off the coast of Florida , where they were on vacation. stories.title: Airing of Bafta racial slur breached BBC standards, corporation finds

The Tourette syndrome campaigner John Davidson could be heard shouting the slur as the Sinners stars Michael B Jordan and Delroy Lindo presented the award for special visual effects during the ceremony at London’s Royal Festival

stories.title: ‘We can’t increase prices any more’: UK hospitality firms hit by cost triple blow
stories.summary: Struggling pubs reel from rising business rates, wages and energy bills, with customers at limit of what they will pay Nick Evans is staring in vain at columns of numbers, trying to make them add up to a profit. He is a co-owner of the Old Crown Coaching Inn in Faringdon, Oxfordshire, a pub and hotel whose rich history is etched into its crooked wooden beams and cosy snugs. Oliver Cromwell stayed here in 1645. A room believed to have been used by the notoriously severe “hanging judge” Lord Jeffr

Until 1991, this cattle town of a few hundred people was a compound known as Colonia Dignidad. stories.title: Close Brothers shares surge after UK bank says it can ‘comfortably absorb’ cost of car finance compensation
stories.summary: Specialist lender says it expects its slice of £9.1bn compensation set by FCA to be about £320m Close Brothers shares surged on Wednesday after the UK bank declared it could “comfortably absorb” its slice of a £9.1bn compensation bill over the motor finance scandal, hours after one of its rivals announced it was selling its UK operations over looming costs. The specialist lender said it expected the final terms of the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) compensation scheme to cost roughly £320m,

stories.title: North Korea fires ballistic missiles toward sea

stories.title: Gunman killed in shootout near Israel's Istanbul consulate

stories.summary: Like many freelancers, Shelly Kittleson often worked on a shoestring budget and without the protections afforded by large news organisations to staff.

stories.title: A londoni rendőrség szerint egy volt Meta-alkalmazott 30 ezer privát fényképet töltött le a Facebookról
stories.summary: A férfit tavaly novemberben letartóztatták, majd óvadék ellenében szabadon engedték.

stories.title: Az ember ilyen messze még nem volt a Földtől, de ha már arra járt, lőtt néhány történelmi fényképet
stories.summary: Képek a Holdról, amit eddig senki nem láthatott. Földnyugta a Hold túloldaláról. Egy különleges napfogyatkozás. Gyűjtemény az Artemis II legénységének fényképeiből.

stories.title: Stabil kétharmadot becsül a Tiszának a Medián
stories.summary: A közvéleménykutató szerint a Tisza Pártnak 138-143 képviselője lehet az országgyűlésben, a Fidesz 49-55 képviselői helyre számíthat.

stories.title: A 900 fős Versenden tartja országos kampányzáróját a DK
stories.summary: Azt írták, itt a DK már megmutatta, hogy le lehet győzni a kormánypártot.

stories.title: Szabadon engedtek egy Irakban elrabolt amerikai újságírót
stories.summary: Shelly Kittlesont március 31-én rabolták el, azonnal el kell hagynia Irakot.

stories.title: Az Országos Roma Önkormányzat elnöke szerint legitim adományokat ajánlott fel a debreceni alpolgármesternek
stories.summary: Aba-Horváth István nem cáfolta, hogy az ő hangja hallható azon a felvételen, amelyen ötszáz élelmiszercsomag húsvét előtti kiosztását ajánlja fel a fideszes képviselőjelöltnek, hogy „segítsen”. Szerinte a csomagokat rászorulók kapták.

stories.title: Délután 4 óráig töröltetheti magát a választási névjegyzékből az, aki mégis itthon szavazna
stories.summary: Már csak pár óra maradt.

stories.title: Meghalt Czigány György, a Ki nyer ma? műsorvezetője
stories.summary: A költő, író, rádiós és televízió újságíró 94 éves volt.

stories.title: Demjén Ferenc letiltatott a YouTube-ról egy NER-t kifigurázó paródiavideót
stories.summary: A Mi vagyunk a NER címre hallgató MI-videó egy ponton Demjén Ferenc dalába fordul át, az énekes ezért szerzői jogokra hivatkozva eltávolíttatta azt.

stories.title: Kinek van ideje sétálni és nézelődni? – Földényi F. László könyvéről beszélgetünk a Telex Olvasóklubban
stories.summary: Áprilisi vendégünk Závada Péter költő, esztéta, drámaíró, műfordító.

stories.title: Nem az NGM szervezte Nagy Mártonék ausztriai kiruccanását, ahogy azt a miniszter korábban állította
stories.summary: A Transparency International adatigénylést adott be a Nemzetgazdasági Minisztériumhoz, de csak azt a választ kapták, nem ők az adatkezelők, mert az Államadósság Kezelő Központ szervezte az utat.

stories.title: A Kutyapárt a feketekampány részének tartja Mérő Vera és Kőszegi Tamás MKKP-filmjét
stories.summary: Az ebek ura című filmben a párthoz korábban kötődő emberek szerint a párt feladta a korábbi értékeit. Kovács Gergely azt mondta, akkor keresték meg, amikor már elkészült a film.

stories.title: Egy újabb NNI-nyomozó beszélt a rendőrség problémáiról, koncepciós eljárásokról, politikai befolyásról
stories.summary: Virág Norman Viktor nemrég mondott fel, mert azt látta, hogy amit ő rendőri munkának gondolt, azt valójában a független sajtó végezte el.

stories.title: Úgy buzdít választásra Wáberer György, hogy maradtak az államhoz kötődő pozíciói
stories.summary: Korábban miniszterelnöki megbízott és kormánybiztos is volt az ország egyik leggazdagabb embere, most úgy buzdít az Oroszország és Európa közti választásra, hogy például még a MÁV igazgatósági tagja.

stories.title: Pollster estimates strong two-thirds majority for Tisza
stories.summary: According to Medián's survey, the Tisza Party could win between 138 and 143 seats in Parliament, while Fidesz can expect to win 49 to 55 seats.

stories.title: Jogerősen is elítélték a pécsi Volvo-botrány vádlottjait, egyiküket bilincsben vitték el a tárgyalóteremből
stories.summary: Gazdasági csalásért és pénzmosásért jogerősen bűnösnek mondta ki a Pécsi Ítélőtábla a másod- és negyedrendű vádlottakat.

stories.title: A bíróság szerint alkalmatlan volt RFK Jr. jelöltje, ezért a miniszter átírta a vakcinabizottság alkalmassági elvárását
stories.summary: A bíró szerint 15 jelöltből csak hatnak volt releváns tapasztalata.

stories.title: Fake Australian, Chinese and Brazilian police stations: BBC goes inside a seized scam compound
stories.summary: Almost nothing was known about the Royal Hill casino until the Thai military took control of it in December.

stories.title: Russian strike on Odesa kills three, including child
stories.summary: A Russian overnight attack on Ukraine’s port city of Odesa killed three people, including a child, and left several others injured, authorities said.

stories.title: Russian drone strike on bus stop in central Ukraine kills three and injures 12
stories.summary: The Russian drone targeted a moving bus deliberately as it was pulling in to a stop to allow waiting passengers on board, officials said.

stories.title: Bangladesh launches measles vaccination drive as child death toll passes 100
stories.summary: UN assists in emergency vaccination drive as country battles worst surge in cases in years amid fall in vaccination rates Bangladesh is battling its worse measles outbreak in years, with more than 100 children dead amid a rise in unvaccinated infants. The government, in partnership with the United Nations, has begun conducting an emergency measles-rubella vaccination drive for children across the country, after more than 900 cases were confirmed since March. Continue reading...

stories.title: Can small nuclear reactors solve Europe's energy woes?
stories.summary: Supporters say modular reactors are key to meeting energy goals, but critics say they're a costly distraction.

stories.title: One in five young Germans plan to leave the country
stories.summary: A recent study shows that as many as one in five Germans between the ages of 14 and 29 are actively thinking about moving to another country — and 40% plan to do so in the long term.

stories.title: How to insulate your household from soaring energy bills
stories.summary: In times of skyrocketing energy prices, record-breaking heat waves and blackouts, producing electricity at home looks more appealing than ever.

stories.title: South Korea turns to EU to revive talks with the North
stories.summary: Seoul's unification minister has urged Brussels to act as mediator in North-South discussions, but Pyongyang still seems to show little appetite for interaction.

stories.title: Nasa’s Artemis II crew to reach unseen far side of the Moon on flyby
stories.summary: NASA’s Artemis II crew are expected to reach their destination on Monday where the four astronauts aboard the Orion will – for the first time – look at the lunar far side with the naked eye. The fly-by will last approximately six hours before the astronauts head back home.

stories.title: How can Ukraine help unlock the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump threatened to unleash "hell" on Iran if it failed to meet his Tuesday deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to all shipping. Can Ukraine help unblock it?

stories.title: Trump warns Iran it could be destroyed 'in one night' as deadline nears
stories.summary: Donald Trump has once again escalated threats against Iran, warning the country could be “taken out in one night” as the US deadline for Tehran approaches. Iran has rejected a proposed ceasefire, insisting on a permanent end to the war, while fighting continues to intensify across the region.

stories.title: China is winning one AI race, the US another - but either might pull ahead
stories.summary: Both sides don't want to let their rival dominate. And the competition may yet be transformed further.

stories.title: Easter festivities muted for Syrian Christians after recent violence
stories.summary: Syrian Christians saw their Easter celebrations confined to church prayers after armed groups attacked the town of Al-Suqaylabiyah in Hama on 27 March. Euronews spoke to the faithful in the country on one of Christianity's most important holidays.

stories.title: UAE tells Euronews ceasefire not enough for Iran war solution, says 'no trust' in Tehran regime
stories.summary: In a briefing to Euronews, the UAE president's diplomatic advisor called for a durable regional security solution beyond a ceasefire with the US as central to the Gulf’s security, as “there is no trust” in the current Iranian regime, but also sees a “more prominent Israeli influence in the Gulf.”

stories.title: French TGV train collides with a truck, killing one and injuring 27
stories.summary: The TGV driver was killed and 27 people were injured in the accident, which occurred at a road crossing on Tuesday morning.

stories.title: Centauros: Greece's anti-drone system that changes 'war of drones' at low cost
stories.summary: The anti-drone system, named after a mythical creature symbolising untamed forces of nature, uses electronic warfare technology to detect and neutralise UAVs without munitions.

stories.title: Iran war and energy supply disruptions accelerate South Caucasus collaboration
stories.summary: When it comes to connecting the continents of Asia and Europe, Azerbaijan and Georgia play a vital role in the transit of energy. The leaders of both countries have outlined how they will work together to broaden the Middle Corridor.

stories.title: Chilean woman accused of Pinochet-era kidnaps loses Australia extradition battle
stories.summary: Adriana Rivas moved from Chile to Australia in the 1970s, working there as a nanny and cleaner.

stories.title: Three gunmen 'neutralised' in shooting incident near Israeli consulate in Istanbul
stories.summary: Authorities said a comprehensive investigation is being carried out with law enforcement units under the coordination of the chief public prosecutor's office.

stories.title: Thailand PM urges working from home amid fears over energy crisis
stories.summary: Anutin Charnvirakul encourages measures such as home working and carpooling as country is reliant on oil imports Thailand’s prime minister, Anutin Charnvirakul, has called on the public to conserve energy, urging work-from-home measures and carpooling, as he warned of the impact of the conflict in the Middle East. In a statement posted on social media, Anutin said Thailand was exposed to the crisis because of its reliance on imported oil and gas, and the country could not be complacent. Continue
